title: Man allegedly stole nearly $2,000 from Medford church donation boxes, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Michael Bagley, 60, was arrested for allegedly stealing nearly $2,000 from Medford church donation boxes.
Bagley faces multiple charges, including larceny over $1,200 and possession of a burglarious instrument.
He is suspected of similar thefts in surrounding areas and is due back in court on Aug. 26.

title: Celtics guard Derrick White is taking a basketball job with his alma mater at Colorado
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics guard Derrick White appointed as the President of Basketball Strategy at the University of Colorado.
White will assist with NIL, athlete development, and donor relations, and donate $2 million to the program.
White previously played for Colorado Buffaloes, averaging 18.1 points during his lone 2016-17 season.

title: Celtics reportedly targeting Evan Mobley after falling short in Giannis trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics eyeing Evan Mobley after unsuccessful bid for Giannis Antetokounmpo, per Sports Illustrated’s Chris Mannix.
Mobley averaged 18.2 points, 9.0 rebounds, 3.6 assists for the Cavs during the 2025-26 NBA season.
Trade discussions involve Jaylen Brown, complicating potential deal due to salary differences with the Cleveland Cavaliers.

title: Travis Kelce lookalike allegedly stole purse, credit cards, New Hampshire police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: New Hampshire police are searching for a suspect resembling NFL player Travis Kelce, who allegedly stole a purse.
The suspect reportedly took a purse from a vehicle and used the credit cards inside at a Best Buy.
Police jokingly noted the suspect's resemblance to Kelce and urge the public to identify him by contacting Officer Prince.

title: Plane lands at Logan after captain experiences ‘medical emergency,’ authorities say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An Air Canada flight landed safely at Logan Airport after the captain experienced a medical emergency mid-flight.
The co-pilot took over and landed the plane, which was en route from Newark to Halifax with 61 passengers.
The captain was taken to Mass General Brigham hospital; Air Canada is arranging onward travel for passengers.

title: 5 Bruins takeaways from Don Sweeney’s pre-draft press conference
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney is open to participating in trade discussions to improve the roster ahead of the NHL Draft.
Sweeney is considering trading the team's 2026 first-round pick for an impact player to support core players Pastrnak and McAvoy.
Negotiations continue with free agents Viktor Arvidsson and Andrew Peeke, with a focus on balancing individual contract interests.

title: ‘Unethical’: Jaylen Brown slams ESPN, Stephen A. Smith in latest exchange of barbs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticized ESPN and Stephen A. Smith for their coverage, calling ESPN unethical in a recent social media post.
Brown's comments originated from ongoing tensions with Stephen A. Smith since 2024, involving marketability and anonymous sourcing disputes.
The fallout follows trade rumors of Brown being a central piece in a failed deal for Giannis Antetokounmpo, heightening speculation about his future.

title: Review and setlist: The Strokes’ appeal isn’t ‘hard to explain’ even 20 years later
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The Strokes performed at TD Garden in Boston for the first time in nearly two decades on June 23, 2026.
The band is touring for their upcoming seventh album 'Reality Awaits', set to release in July 2026.
Thundercat and Hamilton Leithauser opened the show, with Leithauser joining The Strokes for an encore cover.

title: Davio’s Northern Italian Steakhouse to close Chestnut Hill restaurant
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Davio's Northern Italian Steakhouse in Chestnut Hill will close on June 28 after 13 years at The Street.
The closure is due to a change in ownership at the adjoining Showcase SuperLux, not due to business issues.
Owner Steve DiFillippo is exploring options to reopen in the South Shore area while other Massachusetts locations remain open.

title: National group alleges ‘anti-Muslim hate campaign’ in Newton
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: CAIR-MA alleges an anti-Muslim campaign emerged after a Newton Eid al-Adha celebration held at City Hall on May 30.
A petition sent to Mayor Marc Laredo by 17 people allegedly raised baseless allegations connecting Muslims to terrorism.
Mayor Laredo affirmed Newton's support for all religious events and denied any discrimination in public space rentals.
